preamble: The shift from 'bʲ' to 'v' is a common form of lenition for voiced palatalized bilabials. The stop becomes a voiced labiodental fricative, often passing through a voiced bilabial fricative [βʲ] stage.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
